name: Bittersweet/White Chocolate Bar Cookies/pt 1
recipeIngredient: 3 1/2 ounces Almond paste, cut into small pieces
2 ounces Bittersweet chocolate or semisweet chocolate, finely grated
recipeInstructions:
The inspiration for this recipe was a little package of chocolate cookies I bought from a pastry shop in Geneva many years ago. The bar-shaped cookies had a rich almond and chocolate flavor and were coated in a bittersweet chocolate glaze. To complete the dramatic professional look, they were drizzled with white chocolate stripes. My version of these cookies looks and tastes quite similar to the ones I remember.
1. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
2. In a bowl, whisk together flour, cocoa powder, baking powder, and salt.
3. In another bowl, beat egg, almond paste, and almond extract until combined.
4. Gradually add dry ingredients to wet ingredients, stirring until just mixed.
5. Roll dough into 1-inch balls and place on a parchment-lined baking sheet.
6. Bake for 12-15 minutes or until golden brown.
7. Remove from oven and let cool completely on the baking sheet.
8. Prepare glazes:
- Bittersweet chocolate glaze: Melt shortening, bittersweet chocolate, and unsweetened chocolate together in a heatproof bowl set over a saucepan of simmering water. Stir until smooth.
- White chocolate glaze: Melt shortening and white chocolate together in a heatproof bowl set over a saucepan of simmering water. Stir until smooth.
SEE PART 2
Makes about 40 2-3/4 inch bar cookies.
